title: State Preserves a Nominal Judiciary While Dismantling Judicial Independence
description: A state retains courts in name but restructures them into two-year terms, cuts sitting judges' salaries, and replaces independent discipline with a legislature-controlled removal body. The scenario tests whether Article X's state-judicial-independence floor is concrete enough to treat the arrangement as a democratic-floor violation rather than a formally acceptable judiciary.

details.changes: imposing two-year terms on state trial judges, reducing their salaries mid-term, and abolishing the independent judicial conduct commission in favor of direct legislative discipline
